title: Correctness, Completeness, Optimality, Metrics, and Selection · Behavior Case: Optimality Claim
summary: A solver wins from one timing, path length is compared across different mazes, and explored-cell animation is mistaken for useful work. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the optimality claim increment in solver benchmark and decision report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
State the finite graph and resource assumptions under which a solver will find a path if one exists.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-optimality-claim — Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-solver-evidence-comparison-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_solver_evidence_comparison_5(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-optimality-claim-1n9q20
# Competency: maze-optimality-claim.
# Case 2966: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_optimality_claim_1n9q20(fixtures: int = 24, valid_results: int = 24, solvers: int = 4, changed_cases: int = 8, isolated_metrics: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1n9q20 = "1n9q20-02q9n1-2966"
    assert evidence_variant_1n9q20
    evidence_axes_1n9q20 = (
        "grid1n9q20",
        "topology2966",
        "search02q9n1",
        "event1n9q202966",
        "access29661n9q20",
        "recovery02q9n12966",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1n9q20)) == 6
    if fixtures < 8 or valid_results < 0:
        return False, valid_results, "benchmark-fixtures"
    if valid_results != fixtures:
        return False, valid_results, "invalid-solver-result"
    if solvers < 4 or changed_cases < 4:
        return False, solvers, "solver-coverage"
    if not isolated_metrics:
        return False, changed_cases, "confounded-metrics"
    assert valid_results == fixtures
    return True, valid_results, "defensible-solver-comparison"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Tie shortest or least-cost claims to edge weights, heuristic properties, termination rule, and verified route cost.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Optimality Claim model · Theory Optimality Claim, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Optimality Claim precondition, or invariant that must hold.
